mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary policy, which raises the cost of capital for businesses and consumers, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has begun raising rates, with FEDFUNDS at 0.33% as of t-1mo.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS has increased over the prior 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation erodes real wages and purchasing power, leading to a contraction in consumer spending and corporate profit margins, which ultimately drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL is 288.663 as of t-1mo, indicating high inflation.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the correlation between high CPIAUCSL and negative returns is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate indicates a robust labor market, supporting strong consumer confidence and spending, which translates into healthy corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is 3.6% as of t-1mo, indicating a tight labor market.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the positive signal, or the average return during periods of low unemployment is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous and sustained decline in both retail sales and housing starts indicates a broad-based weakening of consumer demand and investment, signaling an impending economic slowdown or recession that negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending and housing are critical components of aggregate demand.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST changes is not statistically significant in predicting returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects healthy manufacturing and supply chain activity, indicating strong economic output and corporate profitability, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is 104.5853 as of t-2mo, indicating ongoing industrial activity.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of periods where INDPRO has increased over the prior month, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Robust non-farm payrolls growth indicates a strong and expanding labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings improve.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS is 151314.0 as of t-1mo, indicating a large workforce.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the average return during periods of strong payroll growth is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve aggressively raises interest rates in an environment of already high inflation, it signals a strong commitment to disinflation that can significantly slow economic activity and reduce corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is 0.33% and CPIAUCSL is 288.663 as of t-1mo, indicating tightening into high inflation.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the combined effect of rising FEDFUNDS and high CPIAUCSL does not significantly predict negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and accelerating real GDP growth indicates a healthy and expanding economy, leading to higher corporate revenues and profits, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence increases.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the average return during periods of strong GDP growth is not significantly positive.
